summary: Extend cylinder-flow verification to the flexible baffle (fluid-structure interaction)
description:
*About:*

HLX-347 (reactor-channel geometry) is unblocked now that HLX-341 verified the cylinder-flow solver against DFG 2D-3. Mechanical's final design for the flow-control baffle downstream of the obstruction (see Reactor Channel Study Plan rev. C, section 4) is a 0.02 m stainless shim, not the rigid deflector the original concept assumed - it visibly moves in the channel flow during the vendor's bench test. The rigid-obstacle solver in projects/cylinder-flow/ has no notion of a moving boundary, so it cannot represent this. Before the reactor study can use the baffle geometry, we need a solver that couples the fluid to the baffle's own motion and a case that verifies it against a published benchmark - the same standard HLX-341 set for the rigid case.

projects/flag-fsi/ has the mesh and case scaffolding (channel, cylinder, baffle geometry; case registry) but no physics beyond that - geometry.py builds the mesh, cases.py defines the case parameters, and everything else is a stub. The rigid cylinder-flow solver in projects/cylinder-flow/ is verified and frozen (HLX-341) - do not modify it; projects/flag-fsi/ is its own project, reusing only the meshing conventions.

*Scope:*

* Implement the fluid solver, the baffle's structural solver, the mesh-motion/coupling between them, and the case driver in projects/flag-fsi/.
* Validate the production case against a published reference series for the benchmark geometry and parameters pinned in FSI Verification Protocol v1 (see Docs).
* Open a reviewed PR with a verification report in the body.

*Acceptance Criteria:*

* The production case in projects/flag-fsi/ completes and records the benchmark quantities defined in Protocol v1 section 4 as a time series, written the same way HLX-341's coefficients were (helixlab.io.save_timeseries_csv).
* Validation compares a freshly produced run against the reference series checked in under projects/flag-fsi/data/reference/ and reports the deviations. Real recomputation against the file, not hardcoded numbers - same bar HLX-341 set.
* Do not widen the acceptance criteria or change the pinned discretisation/parameters in Protocol v1 to make a failing comparison pass - open a discussion in #proj-flow instead, same as the rigid case.
* PR body carries a short verification report: the measured quantities, their deviations against the reference, and which case/parameters produced them.
* projects/cylinder-flow/ is untouched.

*Notes:*

* This is a genuinely different kind of solver than HLX-341 - the baffle moves, the mesh has to move with it, and the fluid and structure are coupled. Budget real design time for the coupling approach; it is not a drop-in extension of the rigid case.
* The production case is expensive - budget accordingly and use the smoke case in cases.py to iterate.

comments.author: Elena Voss
comments.body: FSI Verification Protocol v1 is in Docs, linked from this ticket. Section 2 pins the geometry and material/flow parameters, section 4 pins which quantities we're validating and where the reference series lives. Treat it as authoritative the same way Protocol v1 governed HLX-341 - if anything elsewhere conflicts with it, the protocol wins.

comments.author: Marcus Chen
comments.body: Heads up that this is a heavier lift than HLX-341 was - that one added verification to an already-working solver. Here you're building the coupled solver itself. I don't have a strong opinion yet on partitioned vs. monolithic coupling for this - there are real tradeoffs and I'd rather you make the call and defend it in the PR than have me hand you one. Same review process as before: feature branch, I review before merge.

comments.author: Priya Sharma
comments.body: Picking this up. projects/flag-fsi/geometry.py already builds the channel+cylinder+baffle mesh with the fluid/solid subdomains tagged (reused the grading approach from cylinder-flow/geometry.py). Starting on the solid side first since I want the baffle's own dynamics right before I couple it to anything.

comments.author: Elena Voss
comments.body: One thing from the vendor bench test worth having in your head while you validate: the shim visibly settles into a large, sustained oscillation once the flow is established - it does not just deflect and hold like a stiffer baffle would. If your solver's baffle just deflects to a fixed position and stays there, that is not matching what we're actually seeing on the bench, whatever the numbers say.

comments.author: Elena Voss
comments.body: Handing this to Priya for now - I'm out most of next week. Everything I know is in the ticket and the protocol.

description: Resolve the flow field through the reactor channel and eventually couple it to the MK-7 kinetics as a transport term. First milestone was a solver we could defend against a published benchmark (HLX-341, done). Mechanical's final baffle design turned out to be flexible, not rigid (see HLX-401) - the reactor geometry work in HLX-347 is now waiting on a verified FSI solver instead.

Build the reactor-channel geometry (channel with periodic obstacles and the flow-control baffle) and its graded mesh, reusing the cylinder-flow meshing helpers. Blocked again: unblocked by HLX-341, now blocked on HLX-401 - the baffle in the final mechanical design is flexible, and we are not building the reactor study on an unverified FSI solver either.

summary: Verify the cylinder-flow solver against the DFG 2D-3 benchmark
description: Verified the rigid cylinder-flow solver against DFG 2D-3. See PR history. Solver in projects/cylinder-flow/ is frozen from here - HLX-401 is a separate project, not a modification of this one.

summary: Lab ops: shared box disk cleanup
description: The shared compute box is at 85% disk. Old run outputs under ~/scratch older than 90 days can go. Not urgent but will be if it's ignored another month.
